task_summary: Added word-boundary guard to scanBareUrls() in apps/api/src/common/throttle/link-limit.ts. A bare scheme (mailto:, tel:, http://, etc.) is now only counted when the character immediately before it is in WORD_BOUNDARY_CHARS and is not '('. This prevents embedded scheme substrings like 'hotel:', 'motel:', or 'xmailto:' from inflating the per-post link count. Tester added 16 new tests (51 total, up from 35) covering all 5 acceptance criteria.
commands_run: npx --yes pnpm@10.0.0 --dir /home/tstephen/repos/worktrees/ms4a-CO1-tester-20260608 --filter @sfus/api exec vitest run src/common/throttle/link-limit.test.ts
final_test_outcomes: AC1 PASS: word-boundary guard verified — charBefore must be in WORD_BOUNDARY_CHARS and not '('
AC2 PASS: hotel:, motel:, xmailto:, a-tel:, inlined_mailto: all return 0
AC3 PASS: mailto:/tel: at string start and after space/newline/tab/>/[ each return 1
AC4 PASS: '[x](mailto:user@example.com)' and '[call](tel:+15551234)' each return 1 (skipPositions not regressed)
AC5 PASS: mixed-body with 4 boundary-preceded evasion schemes returns 4; hotel: + tel: combo returns 1
The 4 other throttle test files require @nestjs/common and dotenv which are absent from the worktree node_modules; those 55 tests confirmed 90/90 passing from the main sfus workspace
cleanup_notes: tester_artifact_input.json is a required tool-input file retained in the artifact directory
No temporary byproducts were created outside the artifact directory
acceptance_criteria_validated: AC1: Bare scheme counted only when charBefore is in WORD_BOUNDARY_CHARS AND is not '(' — PASS
AC2: 'hotel:', 'motel:', embedded 'mailto:' inside a larger word are NOT counted — PASS
AC3: Boundary-anchored 'mailto:user@example.com' and 'tel:+15551234' each counted once — PASS
AC4: Markdown link '[x](mailto:user@example.com)' counted exactly once; skipPositions not regressed — PASS
AC5: Change only reduces false positives; fail-safe direction preserved — PASS

The word-boundary guard is a behavioral refinement reducing false positives in bare-scheme detection
Any docs covering link-count limiter behavior or per-post link limits may now accurately state that bare schemes are only counted at word boundaries
